ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keefe, via ZOOM, counsel for Conservator Debra Dolch.

At the outset, the Court asks Ms. O'Keefe to confirm that the purchase of a Forty Niner jacket is not a misallocation. Counsel advises that Ms. Dolch reported to her that the Conservatee is a fan of the Forty Niners and asked to purchase the jacket. The court gently reminds Ms. O'Keefe that moving forward, the court asks that specific amounts of fees being requested are clearly set-forth in the prayer. The court confirms that the new bond amount that would be approved is $519,000. The court questions Ms. O'Keefe regarding Ms. Dolch's statement to move to a platform of individual securities given the court's position on ETF's and mutual funds. Ms. O'Keefe believes that Ms. Dolch has been very conscientious about working with financial advisor to ensure the best investments for the Conservatee in order to stretch her money. Ms. O'Keefe states that moving forward, Ms. Dolch has decided to switch to any investments that fall within the court's approval. Ms. O'Keefe is advised that the court is following statute 2574, in order to confirm that the type of investments are proper.
The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. Not Reported

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-87-246640 Conservatorship of Angela L. Moss and
PTR-08-291355 The Angela L. Moss Special Needs Trust.

Appearances: Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Debra Dolch (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Dolch stated that all of the assets are in the special needs trust, that the only income in the conservatorship are the social benefits, that in 2002 the Court issued an order that she was no longer required to do accountings in the conservatorship because the income was less than $1,000.00 per month, that every month she pays for the expense of the facility and for special treats that are not provided by the facility, and that the social security payment is now at $1,149.00 a month. Ms. Dolch also stated that the trust and the conservatorship are two separate entities, that she has responsibilities for both, that she has to keep records, that she is the payee representative and that social security audits her every other year. In addition, Ms. Dolch that she meets with her Client each month, spoke of her Client's desire to have specific items, how difficult it is to find these items and how much time it takes looking for these items, and that there is a lot involved in being of service to Ms. Moss. The Fifth Petition for Approval of Fees for Services Rendered by Conservator, etc., and the Amended Seventh Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary O'Keeffe for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee/Conservator).

The Court informed the Parties that it will not grant blanket approval with respect to the investments, that the investments will need to be re-affirmed and Court approved the next time around and that in the future it would like to see why the particular investments chosen are the right investments for this Trust. The Court granted the Third Amendment to the Sixth Account and Report of Trustee, Petition for Settlement, Compensation and for Decrease in Liability on Bond (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 246640 (Conservatorship of Angela L. Moss).

Appearances: Gregory O'Keeffe for and with Debra Dolch.

Mr. O'Keefee and Ms. Dolch explained that the difference between the amount of the sale of the property and the amount transferred to the trust was due to closing costs, payment of deferred fees, payment of capital gains taxes, attorney's fees, the bond premium, legal fees and the pre-need expense. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR#11604. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
